evals.prompt: I'm starting a new R package called finchdata that downloads bird observation data from an online API. I want it to meet rOpenSci's standards from the very beginning. Walk me through how to set it up: structure, files, naming, dependencies, docs, tests, and CI. What conventions do I need to follow?
evals.expected_output: rOpenSci-specific setup guidance: usethis scaffolding, lowercase/snake_case naming, Imports not Depends, modern deps (httr2/jsonlite), roxygen2 with @param/@return/runnable examples, README with CI+coverage+repostatus badges, at least one HTML vignette, testthat with >=75% coverage, tidyverse style (cli not print), GitHub Actions on release+oldrel+devel, NEWS.md, default branch main, CRAN/OSI license, API key in env var (never printed).

evals.prompt:
Here's the DESCRIPTION of my package 'WeatherTools'. Can you review it against rOpenSci packaging standards and tell me what to fix?

```
Package: WeatherTools
Type: Package
Title: Tools for Weather Data in R
Version: 0.1
Author: Jane Doe
Maintainer: Jane Doe <jane@example.com>
Description: This package provides functions to get weather data.
Depends: dplyr, RCurl, rjson
License: What license is it?
```
evals.expected_output: Flags: name not lowercase (WeatherTools), Title has 'in R' and should be Title Case without it, no Authors@R/ORCID, Description starts with 'This package' and is too thin, Depends should be Imports, RCurl->httr2/curl and rjson->jsonlite discouraged deps, missing/invalid License (needs CRAN/OSI), missing URL/BugReports. Separates required vs recommended.
